Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Victor Makras To Plaintiffs Requests For Admission Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Victor Makras And His Attorney
Off Calendar May-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF DIANA WESTNEDGE's Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Victor Makras To Plaintiffs Requests For Admission Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Victor Makras And His Attorney.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. The motion seeks further response only as to Request for Admissions No. 3, whether the Defendant Makras controlled plaintiff's apartment unit. Defendant Makras objected to the request as vague, ambiguous, lacking foundation and argumentative. The objections are valid and are sustained, especially as to the undefined term "controlled". Moreover, Defendant Makras has admitted that plaintiff had exclusive possession of the unit during her tenancy, subject only to the secondary rights of the defendant. That response is sufficient, and the motion is denied.
Sanctions are denied in the interest of justice. Prevailing party shall submit a consolidated proposed order in Word format to the Temporary Judge at jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Victor Makras Doing Business As Makras Real Estate To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Victor Makras Doing Business As Makras Real Estate And His Attorney
Off Calendar May-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF DIANA WESTNEDGE's Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Victor Makras Doing Business As Makras Real Estate To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Victor Makras Doing Business As Makras Real Estate And His Attorney.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Requests for Production Nos. 9 and 10 seek documents concerning tenders of defense, to which Defendant Makras DBA objected on several grounds without substantive response. Temporary Judge Ward found that the defendants "eventually provided an answer on the subject of insurance tenders of defense". Accordingly, the motion is moot as to Requests 9 and 10. Request for Production No. 38 seeks documents concerning correspondence with an insurance company which Defendant Makras DBA agreed to produce as to its "first party claim for a fire loss". Temporary Judge Ward found that the limitation to a "first party claim" did not render the response insufficient. The motion is therefore moot as Requests No. 38. Requests for Production Nos. 53, 54, 55, 56, 57, 58 and 59 seek documents showing Defendant Makras DBA was not negligent, did not breach quiet enjoyment, habitability, good faith and fair dealing, or a contract, did not cause emotional distress, and did not violate the Administrative Code. Defendant Makras 1625 objected on grounds, among others, of oppression and lack of foundation. The objections are valid and are sustained. Temporary Judge Ward likewise found that the requests "were directed to documents supporting a negative proposition" and that defendants' responses were "justifiable and acceptable under the Discovery Act." The motion is therefore denied as to Requests Nos. 53, 54, 55, 56, 57, 58 and 59. The Court also notes that Defendant Makras and Defendant Makras DBA are one legal entity and that duplicate discovery requests addressed to one defendant in two such capacities unnecessarily multiplied the burdens imposed on the defendant and the Court.
Sanctions are denied in the interest of justice.

Prevailing party shall submit a consolidated proposed order in Word format to the Temporary Judge at jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)
